title: Pellet resuspension
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b0gfj","text":"For use in Viral and bacterial isolates, propagation and preparation of stocks.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":11,"length":67}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"B7C2C1B5D3B94459A5E3625258E70CC2","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/Viral-and-bacterial-isolates-propagation-and-prepa-dzs76d"}}}}
steps: Pipette off 1.5 mL using a P1000.
Switch to a P100 and gently remove nearly all of the supernatant. Remainder usually totals ~10 µL.
Add 40 µL water or seawater medium to bring the volume to 50 µL. Remember freshwater versus seawater choice for viral isolate.
Gently vortex the tubes to disrupt the pellets and place them at 4°C overnight to soften. 18:00:00
authors: André . Comeau and Rachel T. Noble
creator: Amy Chan
